objective: Deliver one substantial fork-only optimization release on top of v1.1.18 baseline to improve practical serverless latency/cost efficiency for Qwen3.5 while preserving current working inference behavior.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible fork-local updates across .runpod/hub.json, src/engine_args.py, src/engine.py, src/handler.py, README.md, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Qwen RunPod Hub presets are latency-first by default: `MAX_MODEL_LEN=32768`, `SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP=32768`, safe/balanced `MAX_NUM_SEQS=1`, safe/balanced `MAX_CONCURRENCY=1`, throughput `MAX_NUM_SEQS=2`, throughput `MAX_CONCURRENCY=2`, conservative `MAX_NUM_BATCHED_TOKENS` values, and `ENFORCE_EAGER=true` for safe/balanced.
Qwen engine args include a guardrail that keeps serverless safe/balanced profile on 32k context when `MAX_MODEL_LEN` is not explicitly set, while preserving explicit long-context opt-in behavior.
Startup logs provide actionable cold-start/queue hints when latency-first concurrency is combined with large context.
OpenRouter/OpenAI structured output behavior from v1.1.19 remains unchanged.
README and runtime compatibility docs include explicit production serverless tuning guidance, active worker recommendations, and long-context opt-in instructions.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this optimization release to restore previous preset and startup behavior. Fast operational rollback without rebuild: pin endpoint env to prior values (`MAX_MODEL_LEN=131072`, `SAFE_MAX_MODEL_LEN_CAP=131072`, previous concurrency/seq targets) and remove latency-first overrides. Validate with py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print before redeploy.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only as one release. In production, keep Active Workers >= 1 and Max Workers >= 2; verify p50/p95 latency, queue delay, cold-start rate, and token throughput on representative short prompts before and after rollout.
threat_model: Main risks are (1) reduced long-context headroom by default if operators expect implicit 128k and (2) lower per-worker throughput from conservative concurrency defaults under sustained high load. Mitigations: explicit long-context opt-in envs, throughput preset retained for controlled scale-up, and rollback via env or fork-local revert.
